mnemonics.title: IaaS-PaaS-SaaS Order
mnemonics.content: I P S - 'Infrastructure Provides Solutions' - Remember: IaaS is at the bottom, PaaS in middle, SaaS at top. Think of building a house: IaaS provides land (infrastructure), PaaS provides the blueprint and tools (platform), SaaS provides the ready house (software).

mnemonics.title: Control Scale Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: S-I-P: 'SaaS gives you Software (most control), IaaS gives Infrastructure (least control), PaaS is in between.' The more you go up the stack, the less control but more convenience.

mnemonics.title: Responsibility Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: P-S-I: 'PaaS Developers, SaaS Users, IaaS Administrators' - Remember: At PaaS you manage apps/data, at SaaS you only use the app, at IaaS you manage OS/middleware too.

acronyms.usage: Use when referring to virtualized computing resources like servers, storage, and networking that are provided over the internet. Example: AWS EC2, Google Compute Engine.
Use when referring to a cloud platform that provides a development and deployment environment for developers. Example: Heroku, Google App Engine, Microsoft Azure App Service.
Use when referring to software applications delivered over the internet on a subscription basis. Example: Gmail, Netflix, Salesforce, Microsoft 365.
Use when referring to serverless computing where you only manage code/functions. Example: AWS Lambda, Google Cloud Functions.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a pyramid: IaaS is the wide base (foundation), PaaS is the middle layer (structure), SaaS is the top peak (finished product). The customer responsibility decreases as you go up - draw horizontal lines showing customer manages bottom portion at IaaS, middle at PaaS, and only data at SaaS.

Picture a table: In IaaS, customer manages OS up; in PaaS, manages apps/data; in SaaS, manages only data and user access. Draw boxes showing what the provider handles vs. what the customer handles for each model.

Think of an onion with layers: Outer layer = Hardware (provider manages), Inner layer = Virtualization (provider manages), Next = OS (IaaS customer manages), Next = Runtime (PaaS manages), Inner = Application (SaaS manages). The closer to center = less customer control.

analogies.analogy: Think of IaaS like renting a vacant plot of land. The landlord provides the raw space (infrastructure), but you need to bring your own building materials, hire contractors, and build everything from scratch. You have maximum control but also maximum responsibility.

PaaS is like buying a prefabricated house kit. The builder provides the structure, plumbing, and electrical pre-installed (platform), but you still need to arrange furniture and customize interiors (develop applications). Great for developers who want to focus on coding.

SaaS is like staying in a fully furnished hotel. Everything is ready to use - beds, TV, AC, housekeeping. You just move in and use the service. No maintenance, no setup, just convenience. Examples: Netflix, Gmail, Spotify.

Classic pizza analogy: Making pizza at home = on-premises; Pizza delivery = IaaS (ingredients provided); Restaurant with chef = PaaS (platform to create); Pizza Hut/Takeaway = SaaS (ready-made product). This perfectly illustrates the spectrum of cloud service models.
